info.name: HRMS Backend API — Story-Driven Testing
info.description:
# HRMS Backend API Collection

A **story-driven** Postman collection for the HRMS multi-tenant SaaS backend.

## 🎯 Testing Flow (Story)

1. **Health Check** — Verify server is running
2. **Tenant Setup** — Register a new tenant (creates OWNER_ADMIN)
3. **Auth** — Login as OWNER_ADMIN, get tokens
4. **Org Setup** — Create departments & designations
5. **Employee Onboarding** — Create employees with all roles (HR, MANAGER, EMPLOYEE, ADMIN)
6. **Role Switching** — Login as any role to test RBAC
7. **Attendance** — Clock in/out, view records
8. **Leave** — Apply, approve, reject, cancel
9. **Dashboard** — View role-specific dashboards
10. **Notifications** — Send in-app notifications

## 🔑 Variables Auto-Populated

Test scripts automatically capture and store `accessToken`, `refreshToken`, `tenantId`, `employeeId`, etc. Just run requests in order.

## 🎭 Role Switching

Use the **"Role Switch"** folder to login as different roles. Each login auto-updates the `accessToken` and `X-Tenant-ID` for subsequent requests.

item.name: 🏢 1 — Tenant Registration
item.description: Register a brand-new tenant. This creates the company + OWNER_ADMIN user. Tokens and tenantId are auto-saved.

item.name: 🔐 2 — Auth Flows
item.description: Login, logout, token refresh, password management. Run 'Login as OWNER_ADMIN' first to populate tokens.

exec: console.log('✅ Logged in as OWNER_ADMIN. Token saved.');
console.log('🚪 Logged out. Re-login to continue testing.');

item.name: 🏗️ 3 — Org Setup (Departments & Designations)
item.description: Create departments and designations before onboarding employees. Run as OWNER_ADMIN or ADMIN.

item.name: Batch Create Departments — HR, Finance, Marketing

exec: console.log('🏢 Batch departments created. HR ID=' + json.data[0].id);
console.log('🏷️ Designation "Software Engineer" created. ID=' + json.data.id);

item.name: Batch Create Designations — Manager, HR Lead, Intern

exec: console.log('🏷️ Batch designations created. Manager ID=' + json.data[0].id);

item.name: 👥 4 — Employee Onboarding (all roles)
item.description: Create employees for each role. Run as OWNER_ADMIN. IDs and emails are auto-saved for role switching later.

exec: console.log('👤 ADMIN employee created. ID=' + empId);
console.log('👤 HR employee created. ID=' + empId);
console.log('👤 MANAGER employee created. ID=' + empId);
console.log('👤 Regular EMPLOYEE created. ID=' + empId);

item.name: 🎭 5 — Role Switch (Login as any role)
item.description: Use these to quickly switch between roles. Each login auto-updates the accessToken so all subsequent requests use the new role.

exec: pm.test('Logged in as OWNER_ADMIN — 200', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
pm.test('Logged in as ADMIN — 200', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
pm.test('Logged in as HR — 200', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
pm.test('Logged in as MANAGER — 200', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
pm.test('Logged in as EMPLOYEE — 200', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });

item.name: ⏰ 6 — Attendance (clock in/out & reports)
item.description: Test attendance as EMPLOYEE first (clock in/out), then switch to MANAGER/ADMIN to view reports.

item.name: 🌴 7 — Leave Management
item.description: Full leave lifecycle: Apply (as EMPLOYEE) → Approve/Reject (as MANAGER/HR) → Cancel → Check balance.

item.name: Get Employee Leave Balance by ID
📊 8 — Dashboard (role-specific)
item.description:
Each dashboard endpoint is locked to specific roles. Switch roles in folder 5 before testing.

- Admin dashboard → OWNER_ADMIN, ADMIN, HR
- Manager dashboard → MANAGER
- Employee dashboard → EMPLOYEE

item.name: 🔔 9 — Notifications
item.description: Send in-app notifications. Requires authenticate + tenantResolver.

item.name: 🚫 10 — Error Scenarios (RBAC & Validation)
item.description: Test that your RBAC and validation actually block unauthorized access. These should FAIL with 401/403/422.

exec: console.log('🚫 Correctly blocked: no auth token.');
console.log('🚫 Correctly blocked: EMPLOYEE cannot access admin dashboard.');

request.description: ⚠️ Make sure you are logged in as EMPLOYEE (use Role Switch folder) before running this.

exec: console.log('🚫 Correctly blocked: missing required fields.');
console.log('🚫 Correctly returned 404 for unknown route.');
